items.name: Depart Philadelphia
items.notes: Check bags, get through security early

items.name: Flight to Prague via Frankfurt
items.notes: Overnight flight, arrive PRG June 14 morning

items.name: Arrive Václav Havel Airport
items.notes: Take bus or taxi to accommodation

items.name: Check in and rest
items.notes: Recover from overnight flight

items.name: Old Town Square and Astronomical Clock
items.notes: Clock shows at top of each hour

items.name: Prague Castle
items.notes: Buy tickets online. St. Vitus Cathedral inside.

items.name: Charles Bridge
items.notes: Busy midday, less crowded early morning or evening

items.name: Josefov Jewish Quarter
items.notes: 6 synagogues and Old Jewish Cemetery

items.name: Vinohrady neighborhood walk
items.notes: Peaceful residential area, beautiful architecture

items.name: Vltava River cruise
items.notes: Great views of the city

items.name: Day trip: Kutna Hora Bone Church
items.notes: Sedlec Ossuary - decorations made from 40,000 human bones

items.name: Morning free
items.notes: Pick up souvenirs

items.name: Train to Vienna (Railjet)
items.notes: Book in advance. Scenic route.

items.name: Arrive Vienna Hauptbahnhof
items.notes: U-Bahn to accommodation

items.name: Evening walk along the Ringstrasse
items.notes: Opera House, Parliament, Burgtheater all on this boulevard

items.name: Schonbrunn Palace and Gardens
items.notes: Book Grand Tour tickets online

items.name: Naschmarkt lunch
items.notes: Vienna's famous open-air market

items.name: Kunsthistorisches Museum
items.notes: One of the world's great art museums

items.name: Belvedere Palace
items.notes: Klimt's The Kiss is here

items.name: Coffee at Cafe Central
items.notes: Classic Viennese coffeehouse, order Melange

items.name: St. Stephen's Cathedral
items.notes: Climb the south tower for views

items.name: Prater and Giant Ferris Wheel
items.notes: Historic amusement park, famous Riesenrad

items.name: Train to Budapest (Railjet)
items.notes: Book ahead. Scenic Danube views.

items.name: Arrive Budapest-Keleti
items.notes: Metro or taxi to accommodation

items.name: Szechenyi Chain Bridge at sunset
items.notes: Iconic view of Buda Castle across the Danube

items.name: Buda Castle and Castle Hill
items.notes: Funicular up from Clark Adam ter. Hungarian National Gallery inside.

items.name: Fisherman's Bastion
items.notes: Fairy-tale towers with panoramic Pest views

items.name: Szechenyi Thermal Bath
items.notes: Bring swimsuit. Outdoor pools are iconic.

items.name: Hungarian Parliament Building tour
items.notes: Must book tickets online in advance

items.name: Great Market Hall
items.notes: Paprika, salami, folk embroidery. Get langos upstairs.

items.name: Szimpla Kert ruin bar
items.notes: Iconic ruin bar in the Jewish Quarter

items.name: Hero's Square and City Park
items.notes: Millennium Monument with statues of Hungarian leaders

items.name: Airport and flight home
items.notes: Allow 3 hours for airport

items.name: Arrive Philadelphia
items.notes: Customs, baggage claim, Uber home

items.name: Reading Terminal Market
items.notes: Best cheesesteaks, Amish pretzels, DiNic's roast pork

items.name: Philadelphia Museum of Art
items.notes: Run the Rocky steps

items.name: Independence Hall
items.notes: Where the Declaration of Independence was signed

items.name: Prague Castle
items.notes: Largest ancient castle in the world. St. Vitus Cathedral inside.

items.name: Old Town Square
items.notes: Astronomical Clock performs at top of each hour 9am-9pm

items.name: Charles Bridge
items.notes: 30 Baroque statues. Best at dawn or dusk.

items.name: Josefov Jewish Quarter
items.notes: 6 synagogues, Old Jewish Cemetery. Haunting and beautiful.

items.name: Beer at a local pub
items.notes: Pilsner Urquell or Kozel. Best beer in the world at best prices.

items.name: Sedlec Ossuary Bone Church
items.notes: Decorations made from 40,000 human bones. Unique.

items.name: Schonbrunn Palace
items.notes: Summer residence of Habsburg emperors. Maze in garden.

items.name: Kunsthistorisches Museum
items.notes: World-class art museum. Vermeer, Raphael, Velazquez.

items.name: Belvedere Palace
items.notes: Klimt's The Kiss. Stunning baroque gardens.

items.name: Cafe Central
items.notes: Historic coffeehouse. Order Melange and Apfelstrudel.

items.name: Vienna State Opera Standing Room
items.notes: Standing room tickets sold 80 min before curtain.

items.name: Szechenyi Thermal Bath
items.notes: Bring swimsuit. Outdoor pools open year-round. Iconic yellow building.

items.name: Buda Castle and Castle Hill
items.notes: Funicular from Clark Adam ter. Hungarian National Gallery inside.

items.name: Hungarian Parliament Building
items.notes: Must book online. Crown of St. Stephen on display.

items.name: Fisherman's Bastion
items.notes: Fairy-tale towers. Best panoramic view of Pest.

items.name: Great Market Hall
items.notes: 3 floors. Ground floor: paprika, salami. Upper floor: langos, folk crafts.

items.name: Szimpla Kert ruin bar
items.notes: Original Budapest ruin bar. Jewish Quarter. Quirky decor.

info.title: Cheesesteaks
info.body: At Pat's and Geno's: know your order before stepping up. Say 'wit' for onions, 'witout' for none. Provolone or whiz (Cheez Whiz).

info.title: Wawa
info.body: Wawa is a convenience store that Philadelphians are obsessed with. Get a hoagie or coffee. Never mock Wawa to a local.

info.title: Tipping
info.body: Round up the bill or tip 10%. Say 'zaplatten prosim' to ask for the bill.

info.title: Currency
info.body: Czech Koruna (CZK). Most places accept cards but carry some cash. Use ATMs, not tourist exchange offices.

info.title: Must Try
info.body: Svickova (marinated beef with creamy sauce and bread dumplings), Goulash, Pilsner Urquell or Kozel beer.

info.title: Transit
info.body: Buy a 24h or 3-day pass. Validate single tickets on the yellow machines. Inspectors check — always have a valid ticket.

info.title: Coffeehouse Culture
info.body: Order a Melange (like cappuccino), Grosser Brauner (espresso with cream), or Einspanner (espresso in a glass with whipped cream). Sit as long as you like.

info.title: Tipping
info.body: Round up or tip 10%. Tell the server the total rather than leaving cash on the table.

info.title: Must Try
info.body: Wiener Schnitzel, Apfelstrudel, Sachertorte (at Hotel Sacher), Tafelspitz (boiled beef), Kasekrainer (cheese sausage from a Wurstelstand).

info.title: Transit
info.body: Vienna's U-Bahn is excellent. Get a 72-hour Vienna City Card for transit and museum discounts. Trams are great for sightseeing.

info.title: Language Note
info.body: Hungarian (Magyar) is unrelated to any Western European language. Locals appreciate any attempt. Most young people in tourist areas speak English.

info.title: Tipping
info.body: 10-15% is expected in restaurants. Say the total including tip when paying. Rounding up is fine for smaller bills.

info.title: Must Try
info.body: Goulash (gulyas - a soup here, not a stew), Langos (fried dough with sour cream and cheese), Kurtoskalacs (chimney cake), Tokaji wine, Palinka (fruit brandy).

info.title: Transit
info.body: Budapest has 4 metro lines. Line 1 (yellow) is the oldest on the continent. Buy a 24h or 72h pass. Always validate — inspectors check frequently.
